methods.description: Process for compiling records of Mexican chironomids from various sources including peer-reviewed articles, books, and collections.

steps.description: Records were compiled from peer reviewed scientific articles, books, and book chapters and, to a lesser extent, unpublished project reports.
steps.searchable_summary: compile chironomid records from scientific literature and reports

steps.description: Specimens of Mexican chironomids are housed in several collections; these records can be accessed using 'Name search' in GBIF.
steps.searchable_summary: access chironomid specimen records using GBIF Name search

methods.description: Classification of Mexican states into zoogeographical regions based on Ashe and O'Connor (2009) and other criteria.

steps.description: Following Ashe and O´Connor (2009) eight major zoogeographical regions are recognized.

Administratively, Mexico is divided in 32 states. Of these the 18 northernmost states are generally regarded as belonging to the Nearctic region, while the remaining 13 southernmost states as belonging to the Neotropical region.
steps.searchable_summary: divide Mexican states into Nearctic and Neotropical regions

methods.description: Arrangement of species checklist alphabetically with additional information on species distribution and habitats.

steps.description: The checklist is arranged alphabetically. Species group names follow the genus and subfamily names.
steps.searchable_summary: arrange species checklist alphabetically by genus and subfamily

steps.description: A short outline with information on the number of species, distribution, and larvae habitats is given for each genus.
steps.searchable_summary: provide outline of species information for each genus
